intro: Quintinia parviflora, also known as the Māpou or Quintinia, is a small tree or shrub native to New Zealand and eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. The bioactive constituents of Quintinia parviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
